vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1381: Dashboard: review/test/ship specialist agents have no 'Restart' context-menu action (and Start can't override model)

plan.title: Dashboard: review/test/ship specialist agents have no 'Restart' context-menu action (and Start can't override model)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: Operators cannot reliably restart a review/test/ship specialist agent from the dashboard. The SessionNode right-click menu does expose a restart submenu with a model picker (added 2026-05-03, commit 50d690377), but for non-work specialists it mis-routes. A sub-reviewer node's restart POSTs the retired /api/specialists/:project/:issueId/reviewer/:role/restart route, which returns HTTP 410 ('per-reviewer restart route retired', commit cfd48b808 / PAN-1048). A test or ship node has no dedicated branch in handleRestartSession and falls through to the work-agent restart path, whose start-fresh branch POSTs /api/agents — an endpoint that only spawns role: 'work' — so restarting a stopped test/ship agent spawns a work agent. For a non-live sub-reviewer the submenu trigger is even labelled 'Start' rather than 'Restart'. The net operator experience matches the issue report: no working Restart for specialists, and model overrides that never take effect.
narratives.Proposal: Fix the routing in handleRestartSession (CommandDeck/index.tsx) and the label in SessionNode.tsx, with no backend changes. (1) A reviewer-node restart routes to POST /api/specialists/:project/:issueId/review/restart — the whole review convoy — because per-axis restart is architecturally retired. (2) A test/ship/merge-node restart routes to the generic, role-agnostic POST /api/agents/:id/restart (restartAgent()), with graceful:false and the optional model override. (3) The restart submenu label reads 'Restart review' for reviewer nodes. The per-session-type endpoint decision is extracted into a pure, unit-tested helper so the otherwise-untestable inline handler is covered. All target endpoints already accept a { model } override, so the model picker works end to end.

autoDecisions.summary: A reviewer-node restart maps to the whole review-convoy restart, not a per-axis restart.
autoDecisions.rationale: Per-reviewer tmux sessions were removed in PAN-1048 (commit cfd48b808); the /reviewer/:role/restart route is retired and returns 410. The review role runs the four code-review-* axes as Agent-tool sub-agents inside a single review-role run, so one axis cannot be restarted or re-modeled independently. Restarting the whole convoy via /review/restart is the only honest equivalent.

autoDecisions.summary: test/ship/merge restart routes through the generic POST /api/agents/:id/restart endpoint.
autoDecisions.rationale: restartAgent() is role-agnostic — it re-launches any agent from its saved state.json with an optional model override — so no new backend route is needed. graceful:false (quick restart) is chosen because test/ship agents have no in-flight work to checkpoint into continue.json.

autoDecisions.summary: No backend changes; the retired /reviewer/:role/restart 410 route is left in place.
autoDecisions.rationale: Every endpoint the fix needs (/review/restart, /api/agents/:id/restart) already accepts a model override. The 410 route is intentionally retained as a defensive guard against stale clients re-establishing legacy machinery, per its own source comment.

autoDecisions.summary: Per-axis model override (restart only one reviewer on a cheaper model) is documented as out of scope.
autoDecisions.rationale: The dashboard cannot set a different model for just one convoy axis without editing roles.review.sub.<subRole>.model config; sub-reviewer Agent-tool sub-agents inherit the review run's model. The issue describes per-axis override as 'ideally', not a hard requirement; the reviewer-node model override applies to the whole review run.

autoDecisions.summary: Restart routing is extracted into a pure, unit-testable helper module.
autoDecisions.rationale: handleRestartSession is an inline useCallback inside CommandDeck/index.tsx and is not unit-testable on its own. A pure helper that maps session type + role to a restart request descriptor lets Vitest verify the routing, satisfying the npm test quality gate.

items.title: Route reviewer-node Restart to the review-convoy restart endpoint

narrative.Action: Fix the reviewer (sub-reviewer) context-menu Restart so it routes to the full review-convoy restart endpoint instead of the retired per-reviewer route. In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/index.tsx, the handleRestartSession branch for sessionType === 'reviewer' currently POSTs /api/specialists/:project/:issueId/reviewer/:role/restart, which returns HTTP 410 ('per-reviewer restart route retired'). Route it instead to POST /api/specialists/:project/:issueId/review/restart (the same endpoint the review coordinator node uses), passing the optional { model } override in the body. Extract the per-session-type restart-endpoint decision into a new pure helper module (src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/restartRouting.ts) so it is unit-testable, and route the review and reviewer branches through it. Add a Vitest unit-test file for the helper.

subItems.title: Choosing restart on a sub-reviewer node sends POST /api/specialists/:project/:issueId/review/restart and never calls the retired /reviewer/:role/restart route.
The model selected in the restart submenu is sent as { model } in the request body; choosing 'Default' sends a body with no model field.
On a 2xx response the toast reports the review was restarted; on a non-2xx response the endpoint's error message is surfaced in an error toast.
A new pure helper module (restartRouting.ts) maps session type 'review' and 'reviewer' (with role) to a restart request descriptor and is covered by unit tests.
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and the frontend npm test suite all pass.

items.title: Route test/ship/merge-node Restart to the generic agent restart endpoint

narrative.Action: Add test/ship/merge handling to the dashboard context-menu restart so these specialist agents restart through the generic, role-agnostic agent restart endpoint. In handleRestartSession (src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/index.tsx), add a branch for sessionType of 'test', 'ship', or 'merge' that POSTs /api/agents/:id/restart with body { model, graceful: false }, using the node's sessionId as :id. Remove the fall-through to the work-agent path for these session types — that path's start-fresh branch POSTs /api/agents, which only accepts role: 'work', so it spawned a work agent when restarting a stopped test/ship agent. Extend the restartRouting.ts helper introduced by the reviewer-restart bead with the test/ship/merge mapping (and confirm the 'work' default still uses the existing resume path), and add unit-test coverage for the new cases.

subItems.title: Choosing restart on a test or ship node sends POST /api/agents/<sessionId>/restart with graceful: false in the body.
The selected model is forwarded as { model }; choosing 'Default' sends no model field and the agent keeps its current model.
Restarting a test/ship/merge node never issues a POST /api/agents request and never spawns a work agent.
A 404 from /api/agents/:id/restart (agent state missing) is surfaced as a clear error toast with no silent fall-through.
The restartRouting.ts helper covers 'test', 'ship', 'merge', and 'work' session types and the new cases are unit-tested.
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and the frontend npm test suite all pass.

items.title: Honest restart-submenu labels per specialist session type

narrative.Action: Make the restart context-menu label honest per session type in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/ProjectTree/SessionNode.tsx. The restartLabel expression is currently `session.type === 'review' ? 'Restart all' : !isLive ? 'Start' : undefined`, so a non-live sub-reviewer's submenu trigger reads 'Start' even though the action restarts the whole review convoy. Add a reviewer case that always reads 'Restart review' regardless of live state, keep 'Restart all' for review, and leave test/ship on the existing `!isLive ? 'Start' : undefined` (which renders as 'Restart' when live) — accurate once the routing beads land. Update any SessionNode/CommandDeck tests that assert the old labels.

subItems.title: A reviewer node's restart submenu trigger reads 'Restart review' whether or not the node is live.
A review coordinator node's restart submenu trigger still reads 'Restart all'.
A test or ship node's restart submenu trigger reads 'Start' when the node has ended and 'Restart' when it is live.
Any SessionNode/CommandDeck tests asserting the previous labels are updated; npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and frontend npm test pass.
